title: verbascum pulvinatum subsp. euryale
intro: Verbascum pulvinatum subsp. euryale, also known as the mullein, is a biennial plant in the figwort family that is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been used to treat a range of health issues. The health benefits of Verbascum pulvinatum subsp. euryale include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to treat skin conditions and promote wound healing.
